text: Imagine a map with several cities connected by roads.

payload.heading_left: What is the simplest way to visualize a graph as a set of connected nodes?

payload.text: Connected graphs have a path between all nodes

text: This can be represented as a graph, where each city is a node and the roads are edges.

payload.text: The minimum spanning tree is used to find the shortest path in a map.

text: An undirected graph is like a two-way road, where you can travel in both directions.
A connected graph is one where you can find a path from any city to any other city.
A spanning tree is a sub-graph that includes all the nodes with the minimum possible number of edges.

payload.text: Spanning trees include all nodes with minimum edges

text: For example, if we have four cities, we can create 16 different spanning trees.
The minimum spanning tree is the one where the sum of the weights of the edges is as minimum as possible.

payload.text: A graph can be simplified into a spanning tree, which includes all nodes with the minimum possible number of edges.

text: This concept is used in various applications, such as finding the shortest path in a map or designing telecommunication networks.
